title: Reserve unique slugs during an online backfill
prompt:
Repair the online slug rollout in `SlugUniqueRollout` without changing its public API or the protected tests. During the expanded phase, both new posts and backfilled legacy posts must receive unique canonical slugs while creates can run concurrently with a batch. Slug generation is deterministic: lowercase ASCII letters and digits, replace each run of other characters with one hyphen, trim edge hyphens, use `post` when nothing remains, and resolve collisions with the lowest available `-2`, `-3`, ... suffix. A slug being assigned by an in-flight backfill counts as unavailable.

The focused defect is that `Store#claim_slug` selects a free slug and yields to its pre-commit hook without reserving that slug. A concurrent `create` can therefore claim the same value before the backfill commits. Make that selection visible to competing writers for the entire in-flight window and release it on both success and failure. Preserve bounded ascending-ID batches with positive integer limits, retry safety after a partially completed batch, no rewrites of already migrated rows, legacy-slug aliases for old lookups, and expanded-phase dual writes for new posts. Finalization must atomically reject missing or duplicate slugs, missing aliases, and canonical slugs that conflict with another post's legacy alias before enabling required/unique slug enforcement; after it succeeds, the legacy no-slug writer must be rejected. Do not modify `protected_tests/`. Use only the Ruby standard library and do not add network access, randomness, wall-clock dependencies, sleeps, or subprocesses to the implementation. Success is `sh protected_tests/verify.sh` passing from the repository root.
